typethis 0.7.0
  • Reference

    • Interop: JSON Schema, ODCS, and OpenAPI
    • Type-checking your whole package
    • Composable type specifications
    • Validators and models
  • Changelog

Wrap a function with input/output type checks

Source: R/typed_function.R
typed_function.Rd

Returns a wrapped version of fn that validates each argument against the spec in arg_specs on every call, and (optionally) the return value against return_spec. Calls that violate a spec raise an informative error before — or just after — fn runs.

Argument specs may be character builtins, predicate functions, or any type_spec (e.g. t_union(), t_list_of()). Coercion can be enabled per call via coerce = TRUE.

All R calling conventions are supported: positional, named, reordered named, mixed, and ... passthrough.

Usage

typed_function(
  fn,
  arg_specs = NULL,
  return_spec = NULL,
  validate = TRUE,
  coerce = FALSE,
  arg_types = NULL,
  return_type = NULL
)

Arguments

fn

The underlying function.

arg_specs

Named list (or character vector) of argument specifications. Names must match argument names of fn.

return_spec

Specification for the return value, or NULL to skip.

validate

If FALSE, type checks are skipped (useful for hot paths).

coerce

If TRUE, arguments that don't match are first run through coerce_type() before assertion.

arg_types, return_type

Deprecated aliases for arg_specs / return_spec. New code should use the latter.

Value

A function with the same formals as fn. Carries arg_specs, return_spec, and typed = TRUE as attributes.

See also

signature() / with_signature() for a separate-then-attach workflow; validate_call() to dry-run validation; is_typed() / get_signature() for introspection.

Other typed functions: as_typed(), as_typed_env(), as_typed_from_roxygen(), default_type_vocabulary(), disable_typed_namespace(), enable_for_package(), enable_typed_namespace(), get_signature(), infer_specs(), is_typed(), parse_param_type(), signature, typed_method(), types(), validate_call(), with_signature()

Examples

add <- typed_function(
  function(x, y) x + y,
  arg_specs = c(x = "numeric", y = "numeric"),
  return_spec = "numeric"
)
add(2, 3)
#> [1] 5
add(x = 2, y = 3)
#> [1] 5
add(y = 3, x = 2)
#> [1] 5

# Argument violation
err <- tryCatch(add("a", "b"), error = function(e) conditionMessage(e))
err
#> [1] "Type error: 'x' must be numeric, got character"

# ... passthrough
total <- typed_function(
  function(x, ...) sum(x, ...),
  arg_specs = c(x = "numeric")
)
total(c(1, NA, 3), na.rm = TRUE)
#> [1] 4

# Coercion
add_lenient <- typed_function(
  function(x, y) x + y,
  arg_specs = c(x = "numeric", y = "numeric"),
  coerce = TRUE
)
add_lenient("5", "3")
#> [1] 8
